refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not combine multiple requirements and avoids specifying how the communication quality is achieved, thus remaining solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication quality monitoring and feedback system to ensure compliance with packet loss criteria
Utilize redundant communication paths to mitigate packet loss impact
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain communication quality independently by adapting to network conditions, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be accurately monitored and parameters adjusted in real-time
Risk that adaptation algorithms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that the agreed network conditions are stable enough for adaptive protocols to be effective
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and feedback mechanisms as separate DPs or sub-functions to enhance modularity and traceability of communication quality management.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network and communication management including adaptive protocols

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving data over the network, essential for adaptive communication protocol adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides data output from the communication module to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as adaptive communication protocol is core to network management; minimal diffusion as it interfaces mainly through Network Link and Telemetry.
Low cohesion since vehicle module focuses on physical control; high diffusion as communication logic would spread across vehicle systems.
Moderate cohesion as teleoperation depends on communication but protocol adaptation is more network-centric; moderate diffusion due to shared responsibilities.
Low cohesion; safety services focus on alerts and safety logic, not communication protocols; high diffusion if included here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it centralizes network communication logic and minimizes diffusion across unrelated modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time network condition data from other modules may cause minor coupling.
If adaptive protocol requires integration with teleoperation commands, interface complexity may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions' directly addresses the FR requirement to support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit. The adaptive protocol logically fulfills the need to maintain packet loss ≤ 1% under agreed network conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential minor coupling due to dependency on real-time network condition data from other modules.
Interface complexity may increase if adaptive protocol requires integration with teleoperation commands.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive and appropriate to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, minimizing diffusion risks. The module interfaces mainly through Network Link and Telemetry, which supports focused responsibility and reduces scattering of the DP across multiple modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by monitoring packet loss metrics through the Telemetry interface and ensuring compliance with the customer-defined packet loss limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and well-justified, with minimal diffusion and high containment scores indicating strong ownership and responsibility alignment.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on real-time network condition data to avoid increased coupling.
Clarify and document interfaces between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to manage potential integration complexity.
Consider defining explicit interface contracts for adaptive protocol adjustments to maintain modularity and reduce diffusion risk.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justification, minimal diffusion risks, and feasible verification. The adaptive communication protocol DP fully satisfies the FR, and the NGHE Solution IM is appropriate for containment with manageable interface complexity. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation at this time.

revise.why: To address recommended revisions, specifically to monitor and manage dependencies on real-time network data and clarify interface contracts to reduce coupling and integration complexity.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol DP fully addresses the FR for teleoperation packet loss requirements.
The NGHE Solution IM boundary is cohesive and appropriate for containing the DP.
Interfaces with Network Link and Teleoperation modules are manageable with proper documentation.
open_risks: Potential minor coupling due to dependency on real-time network condition data.
Interface complexity may increase if adaptive protocol requires integration with teleoperation commands.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss ≤ 1% under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Monitoring reports on dependency management and coupling control.
